calendar.matters:
CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jun-01-2022 Off Calendar

Motion To Enforce Settlement
Off Calendar Apr-22-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
John Browne, 415.421.6700 of the Law Offices of John R. Browne lll appearing via Zoom for Plaintiff Tsungyuan Chang.
Edward Sidel, 415.433.1900 of Copper White & Copper LLP appearing vai Zoom for Defendant Alan Wu Kong Tan.

Hearing held. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: PLAINTIFF TSUNGYUAN CHANG, CHIU-LAN HUANG, MING LIN, SHU-JUNG CHEN, MEI-HSING CHEN, RAKER YU, HUI-CHIN HUANG, GRACE LIN, CHI-CHIN CHOU Motion To Enforce Settlement is GRANTED, in part. Plaintiffs are ordered to sign any and all necessary documents to effectuate the close of escrow. (Settlement Agreement, Section 20.) The payment obligations under the Settlement Agreement shall be made within 45 days following the close of escrow. Plaintiff's request for attorney's fees and costs is denied. Plaintiffs are not the "substantially prevailing party" on their motion to enforce the Settlement Agreement. By the time the instant motion was filed, the parties were ready, and have been ready, to close escrow. Plaintiffs are not entitled to prejudgment interest. Prejudgment interest under CC 3287(a) is only available when a plaintiff is entitled to recover damages. Plaintiff have not established that they were entitled to damages as a result of their partition action. (See Union Pacific Railroad Co. v. Santa Fe Pacific Pipelines, Inc. (2014) 231 Cal.App.4th 134, 200, 202.) Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: J. Murphy; Not Reported. (D501)
